page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, resulting in a configuration of 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. There are zero GPUs bundled with this configuration, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from other tenants. The high memory density relative to the vCPU count makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. Tradeoffs include the complete lack of local storage and hardware acceleration. It is suited for workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring significant memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 CPU architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, providing a dedicated CPU allocation with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, requiring external block storage for data persistence. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable compute performance, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high core counts. This instance is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
